question: Is the musician in the foreground rendered in a Cubist style (characterized by fragmented geometric planes and multiple viewpoints)?
Is the flower seller behind the musician rendered in an Impressionist style (characterized by visible broken brushstrokes and light-focused color dabs)?
Is the billboard above the figures rendered in a Pop Art style (characterized by bold flat colors, hard outlines, and a commercial graphic aesthetic)?
Is the distant mountain range rendered in a traditional Chinese ink wash style (characterized by monochrome ink tones, watery gradients, and calligraphic brushwork)?
Is the cat on the right side rendered in a pixel art style (characterized by clearly visible square pixels and a blocky grid pattern)?
Is the stone fountain beneath the cat rendered in a Baroque style (characterized by ornate sculptural details and dramatic forms)?
Is the wrought iron lamp post at the left edge rendered in an Art Nouveau style (characterized by flowing whiplash curves and botanical motifs)?
Is the Cubist style of the musician visually distinct from the Impressionist style of the flower seller despite their close proximity?
Is the pixel art style of the cat visually distinct from the Baroque style of the fountain it sits upon?
Are all seven distinct art styles (Cubist, Impressionist, Pop Art, Chinese ink wash, pixel art, Baroque, Art Nouveau) simultaneously present and distinguishable in the image?
